item.title: Kashmir
item.dc:creator: Abishek
item.content:encoded:
A poem

Though your winds are cold

Your beauty warms my heart

And at the end of the tunnel

Your magic is worlds apart

Your trees wave to me hello

As the snowflakes fall

You cover me with your snow

And answer my every call

Your rivers flow like a lullaby

That every mind can keep

Sing me nature’s gentle song

And put my troubled heart to sleep

In this valley cradled with peaks

Where clouds embrace the morning snow

My soul has found its home

In Kashmir’s winter glow

Abishek 2025

Kashmir was originally published in Literally Literary on Medium, where people are continuing the conversation by highlighting and responding to this story.

item.title: Loss
item.dc:creator: Abishek
item.content:encoded:
A poem

Where will I go without your love?
The world is dark and deep.
And every moment that passes,
I wish I was asleep.

Where can I walk without my compass?
My only hope, my north star.
And every moment that passes,
I wish you weren’t so far.

Where can I see without your comfort?
My guiding light when times are tough.
And every moment that passes,
I miss your endless love.

Where can I reach without your strength?
My best friend, my hope and guide.
And every moment that passes,
I wish you were by my side.

Loss was originally published in Literally Literary on Medium, where people are continuing the conversation by highlighting and responding to this story.

item.title: Painful Farewell
item.dc:creator: Abishek
item.content:encoded:
A poem

She fell like rain from heaven.

And drove the dark clouds away.

And with her loving heart,

She turned my night to day

Her love was without condition.

A friend who came to stay.

Her love was beyond description.

A guide who showed the way

When the storms descended.

She turned the storm to rain.

Her journey on earth has ended.

But memories still remain

Memories of her sacrifices as a mother.

Which made me who I am.

Through the storms we had to weather.

She helped to make me stand

She taught me right from wrong.

She taught me how to fight.

She told me to never give up.

And always stand for what’s right

I’ll never forget the lessons she taught.

I’ll never forget her smile.

I’ll never forget how she fought.

I’ll never forget her courage through each trial

For in my heart, I feel her rhythm.

In every beat, she’ll be my guide.

The heavens are now blessed with her eternal wisdom.

Where her soul will forever abide

I miss you forever, Mom

@Abishek 2024

Painful Farewell was originally published in Literally Literary on Medium, where people are continuing the conversation by highlighting and responding to this story.

item.title: Hope
item.dc:creator: Abishek
item.content:encoded:
Its time to loose your regrets and sad feelings
Old things have passed away
And Yes Its time for new beginnings
The night has turned to day

So don’t give up and make a wish
Its never too late to fly
’Cause All it takes is one little push
For you to reach the sky

So start your engines with full throttle
Runway 28 is clear
Pick up the radio and announce your arrival
The time to fly is here

item.title: We Will Prevail
item.dc:creator: Abishek
item.content:encoded:
Through the highs and the lows
Through the summer and through the snows
Through clear skies and through the mist
Through the mountains and through the abyss
We will pierce disease’s deathly veil
And, We Will Prevail

© Abishek 202

We Will Prevail was originally published in Literally Literary on Medium, where people are continuing the conversation by highlighting and responding to this story.

item.title: Little Victories
item.dc:creator: Abishek
item.content:encoded:
Life is made of battles lost
Battles lost, not lost forever
To win these battles you must pay the cost
The cost to win, it’s now or never

Take risks, Take chances
Take chances though you’re in doubt
And maybe it’s time for little dances
Dances through the stormy clouds

For in defeats there are still little victories
Little victories you may not see just yet
Yet there’ll come a time to know
to know my friend, the sun has not set

Photo from pixabay

Little Victories was originally published in Poets Unlimited on Medium, where people are continuing the conversation by highlighting and responding to this story.

item.title: New Beginnings
item.dc:creator: Abishek
item.content:encoded:
To old friends, and new beginnings
Both fill my heart with joy
Like a warm ray of sunshine
That spreads across the sky

New Beginnings was originally published in Poets Unlimited on Medium, where people are continuing the conversation by highlighting and responding to this story.

item.title: Fight
item.dc:creator: Abishek
item.content:encoded:
Though my dreams may turn to dust,
I know I won’t stop dreaming.
And Though they say I’ll turn to rust
I know I won’t stop trying.

For I am made of carbon fibre,
Not of brittle steel.
Never satisfied with failure
And I know the wounds will heal

Until I win, I’ll keep on fighting,
Fighting day and night.
And I’ll never care about the times I’ve lost,
When Victory is in sight

Fight was originally published in Poets Unlimited on Medium, where people are continuing the conversation by highlighting and responding to this story.

item.title: Let me dream
item.dc:creator: Abishek
item.content:encoded:
Let me dream of a world without hate
Where love , not money , makes a person great
Where humans can dress the way they like
And not be judged based on what’s outside
Where everyone is just and fair and kind
And voices of reason one shall find

Let me dream of a place
Where there are no borders or race
Where law and order shall abide
And liberty and equality are on your side
Where none hold grudges from the past
Where fear and hate mongering fail to last

Let me dream of a city
Where systems are not designed to help the wealthy
Where justice is blind where love is kind
Where thought and deed are refined

Let me dream of a world without greed
Where dreams come true, where knowledge is free
Where one cares about another’s need
And the only wars are against disease

Do not wake me
Let me dream

Let me dream was originally published in Poets Unlimited on Medium, where people are continuing the conversation by highlighting and responding to this story.

item.title: What is …..
item.dc:creator: Abishek
item.content:encoded:
What is life, without no living?
What is friendship, without no trust?
What is happiness, without no sharing?
What is a dream, without no gust?

What is work, without no challenge ?
What is beauty, without no brain ?
What is fame, without no talent ?
What is success, without no pain ?

What is the sun , without its light?
What is the moon, without the night?
What is a cloud, without no rain?
What is a field, without no grain?

What are branches, without no trees?
What are leaves, without the breeze?
With so many questions unanswered, one more i’ll allow.
What is the heart, without no love?

What is ….. was originally published in Poets Unlimited on Medium, where people are continuing the conversation by highlighting and responding to this story.
